description_long: This is a robust, medieval-style war axe featuring a long, cylindrical wooden handle with a dark, natural finish. The head of the axe is forged from blackened metal and includes a prominent, sharply pointed spike on one side and a heavy, hammer-like poll on the opposite side. The blade is angular and wedge-shaped, designed for both cutting and piercing, with a reinforced socket connecting it securely to the handle. Decorative metal bands encircle the upper portion of the handle, providing both grip and visual interest. The end of the handle is capped with a rounded metal pommel, adding balance and durability to the tool.
description: A heavy, blackened metal war axe with a long wooden handle, pointed spike, and hammer poll, accented by metal bands and a rounded pommel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed spike and the hammer poll aligned horizontally, with the handle and pommel visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the wooden handle, the angular blade, and the decorative metal bands near the head.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the sharp spike extending outward and the rounded pommel at the base of the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the blade's profile, the hammer poll, and the length of the handle with its metal accents.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical wooden handle with a natural finish, reinforced by metal bands for grip and durability.
attribute_blade_design: Angular, wedge-shaped blackened steel blade with a sharply pointed spike on one side and a hammer poll on the other; no beard, functional and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and socketed mounting with visible metal reinforcement at the head-handle junction.
